mx.name: Mexico
mx.desc: Mexico is the largest trading partner of the United States. Trade is highly integrated due to the USMCA agreement, particularly in the automotive and manufacturing sectors.

ca.name: Canada
ca.desc: Canada is the second-largest trading partner of the US. Energy trade is highly prominent, with Canada being the largest supplier of crude oil imports to the US.

cn.name: China
cn.topImport: Computers, Cell Phones, Toys & Games, Electronics
cn.desc: Trade with China remains huge but has shifted due to tariff structures and supply chain diversification. The US maintains a significant, though narrowed, trade deficit with China.

tw.name: Taiwan
tw.topImport: Advanced Semiconductors, Computer Parts, Telecom Equipment
tw.desc: Taiwan is a critical supply chain partner, particularly for advanced microchips. The US imports a massive volume of technology products, leading to a large trade deficit.

vn.name: Vietnam
vn.desc: Vietnam has grown rapidly as a key electronics and consumer goods manufacturing hub, absorbing significant trade flows as companies diversify away from China.

de.name: Germany
de.topExport: Civil Aircraft & Parts, Pharmaceuticals, Medical Equipment
de.desc: Germany is the US's largest European trading partner. Trade centers on advanced manufacturing, machinery, automobiles, and pharmaceutical goods.

kr.name: South Korea
kr.topExport: Crude Petroleum, Semiconductor Machinery, Aircraft Parts
kr.desc: South Korea is a core technology partner. US imports of Korean automobiles and batteries have surged, supported by the KORUS FTA.

jp.name: Japan
jp.topExport: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG), Corn, Civil Aircraft
jp.topImport: Passenger Cars, Auto Parts, Machine Tools, Electronics
jp.desc: US-Japan trade is long-standing, focused heavily on automobiles, machinery, and agricultural products. Japan is a major source of foreign direct investment in the US.

gb.name: United Kingdom
gb.topExport: Civil Aircraft, Gold, Refined Petroleum, Financial Services
gb.desc: The UK is one of the few major partners with which the US has a significant trade surplus. Trade is characterized by aerospace, premium automobiles, and service sectors.

th.name: Thailand
th.topExport: Integrated Circuits, Liquefied Petroleum Gas, Gold
th.topImport: Computer & HDD Parts, Semiconductors, Rubber Products
th.desc: Thailand is a major supplier of computer hardware (such as hard disks) and semiconductor packaging to the US, creating a large trade deficit.

in.name: India
in.topExport: Mineral Fuels, Coal, Rough Diamonds, Aviation Equipment
in.desc: US-India trade is growing rapidly. India is a key exporter of generic drugs, textiles, and information technology services to the US market.

nl.name: Netherlands
nl.topExport: Crude Petroleum, Medical Instruments, Tech Equipment
nl.desc: The Netherlands acts as the 'Gateway to Europe' due to the Port of Rotterdam. The US maintains a surplus, exporting significant crude oil and machinery.

fr.name: France
fr.topExport: Aviation Parts & Turbines, Chemicals, Optical Goods
fr.topImport: Wine & Spirits, Cosmetics, Aircraft, Luxury Goods
fr.desc: US-France trade features heavy collaboration in aviation (Boeing/Airbus supply chains) as well as French exports of premium food, wine, and luxury products.

it.name: Italy
it.topImport: Industrial Machinery, Pharmaceuticals, Cars, Olive Oil & Wine
it.desc: Italy exports high-end industrial machinery, fashion, and food products to the US, alongside significant pharmaceutical shipments.

ie.name: Ireland
ie.desc: Ireland trade is heavily dominated by the pharmaceutical sector. Many US chemical companies export ingredients to Irish subsidiaries which package and re-export finished drugs back to the US.

br.name: Brazil
br.topImport: Crude Oil, Semi-Finished Iron & Steel, Coffee, Aircraft
br.desc: Brazil is the US's main trading partner in South America. The US has a trade surplus, exporting energy products and manufacturing goods while importing raw materials.

ch.name: Switzerland
ch.desc: Switzerland trade is characterized by high-value goods like gold refitting, pharmaceuticals, and luxury watches, resulting in a moderate US deficit.

sg.name: Singapore
sg.topExport: Aviation Turbines, Electronic Circuits, Petroleum Products
sg.desc: Singapore is a key financial and logistics hub in Asia. The US runs a trade surplus here, supported by the US-Singapore Free Trade Agreement.

au.name: Australia
au.desc: Australia trade is supported by a free trade agreement. The US exports finished machinery and vehicles and imports agricultural products and minerals.

sa.name: Saudi Arabia
sa.desc: Saudi Arabia is a key oil supplier. US exports are dominated by machinery, passenger cars, and defense/aerospace products.

za.name: South Africa
za.topImport: Platinum & Noble Metals, Passenger Cars, Iron & Steel
za.desc: South Africa is the US's primary trading partner on the African continent, supplying critical platinum and minerals alongside automotive components.
